observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MTO and MAO in collaboration with 1DOC as the service provider, fast track as part of the newly developed information system, the generation of a complete report for RPT delinquencies, by addressing the glitches encountered in the generation of listing on delinquency taxes and cleansing and/or reconciliation of AR manual records converted into computerized records forming part of the database. This is necessary as reference of Management in the subsequent disposition of real properties.

Thereafter, we recommended that the MTO and MAO to undertake validation of the generated reports on delinquent tax as basis of the periodic reconciliation of RPT/SET receivables, to avoid misstatement of RPT/SET Receivable accounts and as basis for the subsequent collection of taxes.

We recommended that Management secure MOA to be executed by and between the Municipality and the concerned barangay to establish beneficial use of the property and conditions on the use of lot owned by the Municipality.

We further recommended that the MAO and MEO closely coordinate with the Accountant’s Office of DPWH – 1st Eng’g District, Pasig City in order to secure necessary documents namely:  copy of the approved JEV; Certificate of Completion; and Acceptance duly signed by the Municipality or Barangay’s end.

We also recommended that MEO validate the completed infrastructure projects and confirm the beneficial use of the property.

We recommended that Management revise the conditions and/or limitations embodied in the ARO through alignment with the provisions of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 02, s. 2015, dated November 25, 2015.

We further recommended that Management shall use as reference in the computation and payments of overtime claim, the approved DTRs generated by HRIS and/or Biometrics system generated.

We recommended that Management adhere to the requirements of Section 56 of RA No. 11975 or GAA for the FY 2024 and the HRMO to -
Restrict the deductions from salaries of officials and employees based on the prioritization of obligations stated in the GAA in order to avoid the net take home pays of employees fall below the threshold of P5,000.00;
Include among the responsibilities of the Head of HRMO, the certification on all loan applications, through an updated written policy or issuance; and
Thereafter, the Head/HRMO shall limit certification issued for loans availed from PCIMPC, only to those employees whose net take home pay will not result below the P5,000.00 threshold after considering the PCIMPC loan amortization.

We commended Management in surpassing the collections over the last year’s figures.

We still recommended that Management, through the LFC, conduct periodic quarterly review of its collection efficiency to ensure improved performance and higher revenue generation.

We reiterated our recommendations that:
The Municipal Treasurer and the BPLO conduct inspection of the 1,248 business establishments and reconcile their records/lists, and thereafter, require the immediate imposition of business taxes, fees and charges; and
The Municipal Treasurer require the submission of barangay clearances before payment of business taxes and issuance of permits.

We also recommended that the BPLO pursue the checking of the database for the CY 2024 with the information contained in the schedule attached for establishments listed in the Barangays which may not be found in their records for the possibility that these were in operation in the past but per their records on present date have already discontinued business activities. Information generated shall be forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for appropriate action as well.

We recommended the optimal use of the LDRRM Funds by addressing the planned programs and activities under the four thematic areas for Mitigation Fund which are necessary for the attainment of the goals and objectives of the Municipal’s LDRRM Plan.

We recommended that the requirements of Section 11(b) (1) of RA 10121 be observed by requiring the PDRRMO to render the status of implementation for LDRRM-PPAs. This will help provide the Municipality with the information on the status of implementation of the DRRM-PPAs.
We recommended that the GFPS Chairperson formulate the GAD Agenda in accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013, and PCW MC No. 2018-04 dated May 4, 2018.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the required documents, explanations and/or justification in compliance with 
Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the Municipal Administrator direct the Inventory Committee on PPE accounts to complete the physical inventory of property, and conduct the reconciliation with the property and accounting records based on the revised Physical Inventory Plan. Thereafter, require the Committee to render a report upon completion and reconciliation of the PPE accounts within the prescribed timetable.
We further recommended that the MGSO and the MAO to –
Set up an effective system to keep track and capture the related transactions on PPE, maintain complete property cards and PPE ledger cards; reconcile and adjust PPE records regularly, and promptly reconcile the results of the physical count with the accountabilities recorded in the books of accounts and the property cards; and
Submit the reconciled RPCPPE to the Auditor within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, 
Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation to require the MTO to provide the Municipal Accountant’s Office (MAO) with the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectible to serve as basis for the setup of the correct RPT/SET Receivables in the books pursuant to Section 20 of COA Circular No. 2002-003 or MNGAS, Volume I.
We likewise recommended that the MTO and MAO undertake periodic reconciliation of RPT/SET receivables with the reported delinquent taxes and make necessary adjustments to avoid misstatement of RPT/SET Receivables and serve as basis for the subsequent collection of taxes.

We recommended the MTO to request for copies of the bank’s debit and credit memos for submission to the MAO to support the adjustments to the related asset and government equity accounts. Thereafter, the MAO to prepare the necessary entries for the reconciling items upon receipt of the documents from the MTO.

We recommended that Management secure a MOA to be executed by and between the Municipality and the concerned barangay to establish beneficial use of the property and conditions on the use of lot owned by the Municipality.
We further recommended that the MAO and MEO closely coordinate with the Accountant’s Office of DPWH – 1st Eng’g District, Pasig City in order to secure necessary documents namely:  copy of the approved JEV; Certificate of Completion; and Acceptance duly signed by the Municipality or Barangay’s end.
We also recommended that MEO validate the completed infrastructure projects and confirm the beneficial use of the property.

We recommended that Management require:
MGSO and MAO to undertake the reconciliation of RPCI/stock cards with the records of the MAO;
MGSO to -
Regularly update the recording in the stock cards for all the Inventory accounts as basis of reporting the correct amount of Inventory; and
Maintain stock cards for all supplies, and for MAO to update regularly the subsidiary ledgers of supplies as the basis of reporting the correct amount of Inventory.

We recommended Management to conduct analysis and verification of the receivables and exhaust all possible remedies to collect the same, and if proved futile, request for write-off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management revisit the previously approved FinDES MOA with LBP to ensure that the requirements of Section 5.3.5 of COA Circular No. 2021-014 dated December 22, 2021, be considered and that other conditions stated therein which are no longer applicable at the present time can be further modified.
We also recommended that the MTO adhere to the requirements of Section 5.3.5 of COA Circular No. 2021-014 dated December 22, 2021, by requiring the LBP to document the disbursements for payrolls paid thru ATM, with Certified list of successful fund transfers to each payee’s accounts covering CY 2023.

Conduct periodic quarterly review of its collection efficiency to be assured of improved performance and higher revenue generation; and
Use the revenue targets on the real property taxes to further intensify collections therefrom. Avail of the administrative and judicial remedies in enforcing payments particularly in tax delinquencies.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the revision and codification of the Municipality’s revenue rules and regulations in order to come up with an updated revenue code that would facilitate ready reference for the implementers and users.
We also recommended that Management coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to revisit the provisions of 2004 Revenue Ordinance to come up with amendments that would give an updated imposition of tax rates to include the Tax on Delivery Trucks and Vans and clearer interpretation of other revenue provisions.

We reiterated our recommendation that:
The Municipal Treasurer and the BPLO conduct inspection of the 
412 business establishments and reconcile their records/lists, and thereafter, require the immediate imposition of business taxes, fees, and charges; and
The Municipal Treasurer require the submission of barangay clearances before payment of business taxes and issuance of permits.
We also recommended that the BPLO pursue the checking of the database for the CY 2023 with the information contained in the schedule attached for establishments listed in the Barangays which may not be found in their records for the possibility that these were in operation in the past but per their records on present date have already discontinued business activities. Information generated should be forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for appropriate action as well.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan through the Presiding Officer for the enactment of Ordinance on the adoption of SFMV, which is necessary in the general revision of real property assessments.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer, the BPLO and the person-in charge in the assessment of the tax due as well as the approving officer explain the occurrence of P100.00 fixed amount of declared gross receipts for CYs 2018 to 2023 tax period.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer exercise the mandate prescribed in Section 171 of RA No. 7160, to conduct examination of the books of accounts of these business establishments to determine actual gross receipts for CYs 2018 to 2023, compute the tax liabilities, including penalties and charges, and demand immediate payment of taxes.

We recommended the full implementation of LDRRM by addressing the planned programs and activities under the four thematic areas for Mitigation Fund which are necessary for the attainment of the goals and objectives both of the City’s LDRRM Plan and NDRRM Plan.

We recommended that the Pateros DRRMO comply with requirements of Section 11(b) (1) of RA No. 10121 by rendering a Status of Implementation for PPAs funded out of the unexpended DRRMF for CYs 2018 to 2022.

We recommended that Management through the MENRO to study the re-opening of the operation of the MRF in Barangay Sto. Rosario-Kanluran in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003 to effectively address the specific needs of the Municipality and its constituent barangays, particularly in waste minimization and reduction.

We recommended that the Management, as provided in the deliverables of the Contract, initiate appropriate action against the service provider, so that the PATOS will completely deliver the generation of the following reports and likewise address the noted deficiencies:
Summary Report on Tardiness, undertime and overtime of employees; and
Daily Time Records bearing complete data to include as well the noon time log in and outs of the Municipality’s employees.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the required documents, explanations and/or justification in compliance with 
Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We reiterated the following recommendations that:
The Management prioritize the transfer of the title of the acquired/donated lot and provide adequate appropriation to cover payment of capital gains tax, documentary stamp tax , other taxes and fees relevant to the transfer of land title in the name of the Municipality; and
The designated Municipal General Service Officer take appropriate actions to facilitate the registration of purchased untitled land in favor of the Municipality.

5.  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and   Municipal

         requirements in support of liquidation  reports  to  establish  the
         validity and propriety of all expenses incurred.

7.  We  recommended  that  the  BAC  and   Technical   Working   Group
         (TWG)/Secretariat strictly observe the provisions of Sections 32 of
         the
         Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in  evaluating  the  entire  procurement
         process and undertaking the post-qualification activities necessary
         in  determining  the  basis  in  awarding  the  contract   to   the
         contractor/supplier  who  offered   the   Lowest   Calculated   and
         Responsive Bid.

6. We recommended that Management, through the LFC, to -


         a.   Involve the MTO and other Departments concerned in generating
            revenues to meet its targets;


         b.   Require the MTO to accomplish the Quarterly Report of  Income
            to keep track of the status of revenue generation; and


         c.   Require the concerned officials to conduct periodic review of
            the Quarterly Report of Income to ascertain whether  collection
            targets are attained, and  to  provide  assurance  of  improved
            finances for the operations of the Municipality.

8. We still recommended that  Management,  through  the  LFC,  conduct
         periodic quarterly review of its collection  efficiency  to  ensure
         improved performance and higher revenue generation.

6. We recommended that  Management  coordinate  with  the  Sangguniang
         Bayan, through the Presiding  Officer,  for  the  enactment  of  an
         Ordinance on the adoption  of  SFMV,  which  is  necessary  in  the
         general revision of assessments and property classifications.

7. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer –

         • In coordination with  Business  Permit  and  Licensing  Officer,
           conduct inspection of the business establishments that  are  not
           included in the database of the BPLO to  actually  validate  their
           existence;


         • Require the immediate imposition of  business  taxes,  fees  and
           charges and explanation  from  owners/proprietors  on  operating
           without the benefit of business permits; and


         • Strictly enforce the requirement of Section 152 of the LGC where
           barangay  clearances   are   to   be   submitted   by   business
           establishments  before  business  taxes  are  paid  and  permits
           issued.


      8. We also recommended that  the  BPLO  pursue  the  checking  of  the
         database for the CY 2021 with  the  information  contained  in  the
         schedule attached for establishments listed in the Barangays  which
         may not be found in their records for the  possibility  that  these
         were in operation in the past but per their records on present date
         have  already   discontinued   business   activities.   Information
         generated should  be  forwarded  to  the  Municipal  Treasurer  for
         appropriate action as well.

4. We recommended that the  Municipal  Treasurer,  the  BPLO  and  the
        person in-charge in the assessment of the tax due as  well  as  the
        approving Officer, explain the occurrence of P100 fixed  amount  of
        declared gross receipts for CYs 2018 to 2021 tax period.


     5. We also recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  exercise  the
        mandate prescribed in Section  171  of  RA  No.  7160,  to  conduct
        examination  of  the  books   of   accounts   of   these   business
        establishments to determine actual gross receipts for CYs  2018  to
        2021, compute the tax liabilities, including penalties and charges,
        and demand immediate payment of taxes.

6. We recommended that Management require MDRRMO to prepare and submit
       to COA the Status of Implementation of CYs 2016 to 2021  DRRM  Plan
       pursuant to Sections 11(b) (1) and 12  (a)  of  RA  No.  10121,  in
       relation to Section 4, Rule 6, IRR, thereof.

7.  We  recommended  that  the  funding  requirements  for   manpower,
       facilities, maintenance and other  operating  needs  of  PDRRMO  be
       considered in the annual budget, in compliance with Section  12  of
       RA No. 10121 and the NDRRM Plan.

7. We recommended that the GAD Focal Person monitor the implementation
        of GAD activities to  assure  maximum  implementation  thereof  and
        ensure that the planned programs, projects and activities  for  GAD
        and other relevant issues for GAD be implemented in compliance with
        the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

          responsiveness for GAD programs implemented. The same report shall
          be furnished to the Auditor in compliance to the requirement of
          COA Circular No. 2014-001.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended and Management agreed to require the LFC to conduct a BF prior to the preparation of the BP in the preparation of the CY 2016 budget to provide the focus on the objectives and targets for the budget year and arrive at a common understanding on what the Municipal Government aims to achieve and the strategies to be adopted within the expected resources.

We recommended and Management agreed to require for the CY 2016 budget (i) all heads of offices/departments to observe strict compliance with the provision of the information and accomplishment reports as required under the pertinent section of R.A. No. 7160 and the preparation and submission of the BPs using the prescribed budget preparation forms and PPMP; and (ii) the MBO to provide all offices/ departments/ NGAs with the appropriate budget preparation forms to be accomplished and to comprise the BPs.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:

We recommended that management: (i) stop the charging of the payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF; (ii) make proper representation for the payment of the services to the GF subject to the conditions provided under Section 458 (xi) of RA No. 7160; and (iii) ensure that the Special Education Fund be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided for under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction:
Allowances that were given to the teachers was based on the circular of 1997 whereby allowances of P550.00 was charged to SEF and the balance shall be charged to GF amounting to P650.00

Payment of allowances for teachers who render their services as ALS teachers were charged to SEF due to the shortage of teachers. This charging of allowances is allowable under IRR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer prepare  a duly certified list  showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in setting up the beginning balances of RPT and SET Receivables in the books of accounts.  In such manner, the related receivable accounts and deferred liability will be presented fairly in the financial statements;
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of records to establish a realistic balance of Receivables from Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes so that necessary adjustment(s) in the books of accounts can be effected for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and
The Local Chief Executive (LCE), Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Municipal officials exert concerted efforts to intensify collection of long outstanding receivables from Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax amounting to P26,185,273.01 and P31,295,448.33, respectively, including interests thereon.
1.12   We further recommended that Management:
Cause the employment/hiring of additional staff in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the preparation and distribution of notices of delinquency to defaulting taxpayers;
Cause the provision of appropriations for the costs of publication in newspaper of general circulation to publicly advertise the sale or auction of real property owned by delinquent taxpayers; and
Initiate the improvement of the billing system for real property tax on land and improvement to ensure that taxes for both land and improvement are collected adequately from the taxpayers. The law requires the owner of real property to pay corresponding taxes on land and improvement within the due date regardless of the separation of Tax Declaration for land and improvement.
The Municipal Treasurer commented that the 2015 certified list of RPT Receivables is already submitted to the Accounting Office. The list for CYs 2016 and 2017 is still in process of updating and adjustments, and that they requested the LCE to provide/detail additional manpower/staff to expedite the preparation and distribution of Notices of Delinquency, since RPT Section has only two personnel/staff.
The Municipal Assessor commented that he had already transmitted to the Accounting Office the list of 2002 to 2016 delinquent properties for further evaluation and adjustments; and likewise incorporated the land and improvement in the issuance of Real Property Tax Order of Payment (RPTOP) for the Treasurer to collect the taxes at the same time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer reconcile their property records to provide reliable balances of  PPE accounts in the financial statements. The creation of appraisal committee is likewise recommended to assess the physical condition of unrecorded/donated properties; appraise the assets and determine valuation based on prevailing current market value; and prepare a consolidated report on the appraised value of donated properties as basis in recording the assets in the books of accounts.
The Management committed that they will appraise the assets and determine proper valuation of donated properties.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Assessor and the Budget Officer to prioritize the transfer of title of the acquired/donated land and provide adequate appropriation to cover payment of capital gains tax, documentary stamp and other taxes and fees relevant to the transfer of title in the name of the Municipal Government of Pateros, respectively, to establish absolute ownership thereof; and
The Municipal Treasurer as custodian of the land titles maintain real property cards to effectively control and monitor any movement in the land account.

4.6 We recommended that the concerned Municipal officials and the Local School Board review and focus their planned programs, projects and activities to those which are rightfully chargeable against the SEF and ensure a more efficient utilization of resources.

We reiterated our prior recommendation that Management make representation to Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The MSWMB to prepare and formulate the annual plans and programs to support the activities in the attainment of its target as laid down in the Municipality’s 10-year ESWMP, which may result to reduce contract cost on garbage collection and hauling services;
The MSWMB to conduct and integrate the WACS in the 10-Year ESWMP to facilitate the approval of the plan by the NSWMC; and
The re-start of the operation of the two MRF and encourage the creation of cooperatives with the end view of partnering with them in the marketing and selling of the products from MRF.

We recommended that Management direct concerned officials to draft a written policy and guidelines on the implementation and monitoring of its Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan as well as to design/formulate the necessary information and education campaign strategies to inform the public on the benefits and impact to the environment of proper waste segregation and disposal.

We recommended that Management:
Cause the establishment of a Mega MRF to serve the entire Municipality, preferably in a Municipal-owned lot; and
Cause the partnership arrangement of the Municipality/Barangay with the private entities involve in composting/recycling business for the purpose of acquiring equipment for composting and recycling and as the agent in bringing back to the economy the recycled products.
Management made an assurance that addressing this issue shall be their priority concern in the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and Management agreed to require:

The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their respective records on Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and effect adjustments accordingly. 

The different offices of the Municipal Government to conduct actual physical count on PPE under their respective custody and prepare an inventory report showing complete description of the PPE item, model, serial number, property number, acquisition costs, and dates for submission to the Municipal General Services Office for reconciliation with its current inventory reports on PPE.

The Municipal officials concerned to create and constitute a committee on PPE appraisal who in turn shall conduct appraisal of all acquired properties in prior years including donated property for valuation purposes.

Conduct of reconciliation of records on PPE of MA and GSO

To convene with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee for the conduct of appraisal and disposal of all unserviceable properties of Municipality.
observations.reason:
Incomplete documentation

Some items are not yet disposed due to lack of proper documentation as proof of ownership.

Concerned officials to take appropriate actions to facilitate the land titling of the acquired untitled land in favor of the Municipal Government of Pateros to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the concerned Municipal officials furnish the GSIS with a copy of the latest inventory report with complete list of all insurable properties so that the appraisal thereof and the computation of the amount of insurance premiums payable by the Municipality could be readily made. Henceforth, strict compliance with R.A. No. 656 should be observed to ensure that the Municipality will be compensated or indemnified for property damages or losses caused by fire, earthquake and other covered calamities. 

We also recommended concerned personnel/official to exert extra efforts in finding or retrieving the necessary documents to facilitate the enrollment of motor vehicles for insurance coverage with the GSIS.

We recommended that Management use the Petty Cash Fund only for those recurring expenses of the Municipality when it is inconvenient to pay through check pursuant to the specific provision of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended the creation of screening committee prior to the renewal of the 19 contracts for services (consultants) to determine compliance with the qualification standards and to establish the required number of consultants in the Municipality. Management should limit the hiring of consultants only to those with highly specialized, skilled or technical personnel not available in the office. The specific functions, duties, responsibilities and definite expected output of the consultants should be clearly indicated in their respective contracts of services in accordance with Section 517 of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation is noted and will be considered in the future hiring of consultants. Necessary accomplishment report of consultant will be submitted monthly.
observations.reason: Screening committee not yet created though there is a set of Officers determining the required number of consultants for the Municipality; partial compliance on the identification of specific task, duties, responsibilities to be performed and expected outputs.

Management strictly comply with Section 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 in determining development projects to be financed by the 20% Development Fund to support the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.  Further, the utilization of the 20% Development Fund should consider that the PPAs partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and cost of recovery and entrepreneurship.

Concerned officials facilitate the preparation of the CDP and the LDIP to comply with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007 issued by DILG, DBM, NEDA and DOF.  Identification of PPAs for medium term plan will boost Management’s capability to identify development projects that will truly address the socio-economic development and environmental management of the Municipality. 

Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor ensures that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized for the development projects provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.  Attention is invited to Section 5.0 of same Circular.
